name: Martha Shulman's Pumpkin and White Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Navy or Great Northern beans
2 medium Leeks, white part only (washed well and minced)
1 lb Pumpkin or winter squash (peeled and chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak beans overnight in plenty of water to cover.
2. Drain beans and combine with 2 quarts water, onion, and bay leaf in a pot. Bring to a boil and skim off any foam that rises. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour. Add 2 tsp salt; continue to simmer.
3. Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or non-stick skillet over medium-low heat and add the leeks. Cook, stirring, until leeks soften about 5 minutes. Add pumpkin and garlic with 1/4 tsp salt. Continue to cook for 5 minutes, stirring often, until the pumpkin begins to soften.
4. Ladle some stock from the beans into the pot. Stir together and transfer the pumpkin mixture to the bean pot. Cover and simmer 30 to 50 minutes, until the beans are thoroughly tender, even mushy. Taste; adjust salt and add pepper.
5. Remove bay leaf and cloves from onion. Return the onion to the soup. Pass through a food mill with a fine or medium blade and return to heat. Stir in slivered sage gently, stirring. Serve garnished with additional fresh sage.
